title: arales
intro: The arales rank, also known as Araliales, is an order of flowering plants that consists of families with diverse characteristics, often including aromatic or medicinal properties. This order includes various families, such as Orchidaceae, which comprises over 30,000 species of orchids, Euphorbiaceae, a family of plants known for their unique sap and diverse range of species, and Cyperaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly referred to as sedges. The plants within the Araliales order are often found in a wide range of habitats, from tropical to temperate regions. The diversity of families within the Araliales order reflects the adaptability and complexity of plant life.

families.family_desc: Myodocarpaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the Myodocarpa family, endemic to New Zealand.
